title: Synnema thwaitesii
taxonomy: Synnema thwaitesii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a type of shrub or small tree that is native to Sri Lanka. The classification of Synnema thwaitesii places it within the genus Synnema and the family Rubiaceae. This plant is part of the larger clade known as the angiosperms, which includes flowering plants. Synnema thwaitesii is further classified within the kingdom Plantae.

Synnema thwaitesii extracts have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Additionally, the plant's leaves and stems have been used in traditional medicine to treat fever, skin infections, and digestive issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Synnema thwaitesii. Its applications in modern medicine are still being explored and studied by scientists.
